title: Erdős #349 assembly piece: α > 2 fails to be a good pair
informal_statement: The fourth and final assembly piece for the integer_isGoodPair_iff characterization used toward Erdos Problem #349: if alpha > 2 (with t > 0), the pair (t, alpha) is NOT good -- it is false that eventually every integer k is a finite sum of distinct elements of the range of n |-> floor(t*alpha^n). This is a sublemma supporting the integer_isGoodPair_iff characterization, not a resolution of #349 itself (which asks, in general, for which real t, alpha the sequence floor(t*alpha^n) is additively complete -- still genuinely OPEN).
informal_proof_idea: The sequence f(n) = floor(t*alpha^n) grows fast enough (since alpha > 2) that f(n+1) eventually exceeds S(n) + 2, where S(n) is the running partial sum of f(0..n). This creates an unreachable gap: S(n) + 1 cannot be a subset sum of {f(0), ..., f(n), f(n+1), ...} for infinitely many n, since any subset either stays bounded by S(n) (if it avoids f(n+1) and beyond) or overshoots to at least S(n)+2 (if it includes any term >= f(n+1), using monotonicity of f). This contradicts eventual representability of every sufficiently large integer.
formal_statement_pp: theorem erdos_349_alpha_gt_two_not_is_good_pair : ∀ (t α : ℝ), 0 < t → 2 < α → ¬ (∀ᶠ k in Filter.atTop, k ∈ {n : ℤ | ∃ B : Finset ℤ, ↑B ⊆ Set.range (fun n : ℕ ↦ ⌊t * α ^ n⌋) ∧ n = ∑ i ∈ B, i})

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop; root_statement_hash confirmed byte-identical to the upstream source hash cited in the sibling repo's own provenance comment

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this packet proves only the last of four infrastructure sublemmas toward the integer_isGoodPair_iff characterization for Erdos #349's integer case -- it does not resolve #349's general open question. Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 65b19a8e, problem_version 50945aea-f5ce-48e7-8580-8117df80b70f), a single Solve step (proof_format raw_lean_block to preserve the bullet/case-split structure), kernel_verified on the FIRST attempt despite being the largest single-theorem proof transported in this corpus's frontier lane so far (118 LOC, 91 tactics, involving Filter.Tendsto/geometric series/field_simp over reals) -- notably the sibling repo's own tracked episode needed 3 attempts to reach the same result, and this repo hit a 60-second wall-clock timeout on a much smaller, related field_simp-bearing proof earlier this session (see BLOCKERS.md), so this success was not a given. root_statement_hash confirmed byte-identical to the hash cited in the sibling repo's own source comment. Independently re-verified in THIS environment's own pinned toolchain rather than treated as evidence merely because the sibling repo (F:\Github\mnehmos.llm-driven-proof-search.environment\ErdosProblems\erdos-349\proof\Erdos349_alpha_gt_two_not_isGoodPair.lean) already reports it kernel_verified there -- per this repo's evidence policy. training.eligibility: quarantined per the default-quarantine policy for open_problem_related packets pending real external review. Seventh packet in the frontier/erdos lane; 4/7 of the integer_isGoodPair_iff cluster now packetized (exists_finset_sum_two_pow, one_two_is_good_pair, alpha_le_one_not_is_good_pair, alpha_gt_two_not_is_good_pair).
